concepts.title: US Hegemony in World Politics
concepts.description: The concept of US hegemony refers to the dominant position of the United States in the global political economy and international relations. The US maintains its position through its economic, military, and cultural power, which allows it to shape global policies and institutions.

concepts.title: Key Features of US Hegemony
concepts.description: The key features of US hegemony include its military superiority, economic dominance, cultural influence, and its role in shaping global institutions such as the IMF, World Bank, and WTO.

concepts.title: US Hegemony and Global Governance
concepts.description: The US exercises significant influence over global governance through its ability to shape international institutions, set global policies, and enforce its interests through its economic and military power.

concepts.title: Mnemonics for US Hegemony
concepts.description: Mnemonics for US Hegemony: 'MICE' - Military, International Institutions, Cultural influence, and Economic power.

concepts.title: Confusing US Hegemony with Imperialism
concepts.description: Students often confuse US hegemony with imperialism. However, hegemony is a voluntary relationship between the hegemon and other states, whereas imperialism is a relationship of dominance and subjugation. The key difference is that hegemony is based on consent, whereas imperialism is based on coercion.

concepts.title: Key International Institutions
concepts.description: Key international institutions to remember in the context of US hegemony include the United Nations, IMF, World Bank, WTO, and NATO.

concepts.title: Challenges to US Hegemony
concepts.description: The challenges to US hegemony include the rise of other global powers such as China, the increasing influence of regional organizations, and the growing resistance to US policies and actions from non-state actors and other nations.

concepts.title: Impact of US Hegemony on Global Politics
concepts.description: The impact of US hegemony on global politics includes the promotion of liberal values and democracy, the establishment of a rules-based international order, and the creation of new global alliances and institutions.
